# UC-22.39.4 · Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions

## Description

Reads the structured material-fact fields of every regulator submission for an incident and alerts when the same incident has been described with divergent numbers or data classes. A diverging narrative across regulators is a pattern that escalates enforcement severity.

## Value

Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.

## Implementation

(1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found. Indexes required: `index=notifications`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=regulator:submission`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions** — Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.

Documented **Data sources**: regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: notifications; **sourcetype**: regulator:submission. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=notifications, sourcetype=regulator:submission, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **incident_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: data_set, data_uniques, incident_id, subjects_set, subjects_uniques.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ES Incident Review queue and the GRC system; verify notification timers and material facts against the official breach and regulator submission record.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'.

## SPL

```spl
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
```

## Visualization

Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'. Key fields: `data_set`, `data_uniques`, `incident_id`, `subjects_set`, `subjects_uniques`.
